tasks.description: Tools canvas data layer — migration for tool_sections table + ALTER tools with position_x/position_y/status columns, TypeScript types, API routes (tool-sections CRUD), update tools API for new columns, client wrappers
acceptance_criteria: New migration file (023) creates tool_sections table: id UUID PK, workspace_id UUID FK, name TEXT, description TEXT, position_x NUMERIC DEFAULT 0, position_y NUMERIC DEFAULT 0, width NUMERIC DEFAULT 600, height NUMERIC DEFAULT 400, created_at, updated_at. RLS via can_access_workspace(). update_updated_at trigger. Index on workspace_id.
Same migration ALTERs tools table: ADD COLUMN position_x NUMERIC DEFAULT 0, position_y NUMERIC DEFAULT 0, status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'active' CHECK(status IN ('active','considering','cancelled'))
New ToolSection interface in types/database.ts matching all columns. Tool interface updated with position_x, position_y, status fields.
New API route: POST /api/v1/tool-sections (create) + GET by workspace_id in same route. Follows existing pattern (successResponse/errorResponse envelope). Requires workspace_id.
New API route: PATCH/DELETE /api/v1/tool-sections/[id]. PATCH supports name, description, position_x, position_y, width, height. DELETE with 0-row detection → 404.
Existing tools API routes (POST + PATCH) updated to accept position_x, position_y, status in editable fields
Client wrappers in client.ts: fetchToolSections, createToolSection, updateToolSection, deleteToolSection. All use apiFetch<T>() pattern.

patterns_to_follow: Migration pattern: CREATE TABLE + RLS policies + update_updated_at trigger + workspace_id index (see 013_tools.sql)
API envelope: successResponse(data) / errorResponse(code, message) from lib/api/response.ts
apiFetch<T>() unwraps envelope — client wrappers return apiFetch<T>(url) directly
PATCH editable fields: const EDITABLE_FIELDS = [...] then pick from body
logActivity() fire-and-forget with void prefix on all mutations
learnings_relevant: apiFetch unwraps envelope — do NOT add nested property access
Supabase RLS silent mutations — check data is null after mutations, return 403

tasks.special_instructions: Existing tools table has: id, workspace_id, name, description, category, vendor, url, cost_per_month, created_at, updated_at. Use ALTER TABLE to add 3 new columns. tool_sections follows the sections/stages pattern (position + size for React Flow container nodes). The tools canvas is workspace-level (NOT tab-based) — tool_sections reference workspace_id directly, not tab_id. Status enum uses TEXT + CHECK constraint (not a pg enum) — simpler for a 3-value column. Follow stages/route.ts as the closest model for tool-sections API routes.

tasks.description: Gap analysis not_configured message: replace 'redeploy' with SaaS-appropriate language (same fix as IMP-064 in ai-analysis-view.tsx but for gap-analysis-view.tsx)
acceptance_criteria: gap-analysis-view.tsx not_configured block no longer contains the word 'redeploy'
Replacement text is SaaS-appropriate (e.g., 'Contact your administrator to configure the OPENROUTER_API_KEY.' or similar to what IMP-064 used in ai-analysis-view.tsx)

patterns_to_follow: IMP-064 already fixed this exact text in ai-analysis-view.tsx — read that file's not_configured block and use the same wording

tasks.special_instructions: The offending text is at line ~325: 'to your environment variables and redeploy.' Read ai-analysis-view.tsx's not_configured block for the replacement wording used by IMP-064.

tasks.description: Fix pre-existing hydration mismatch on AI analysis date format — server renders different date string than client. Use consistent formatting.
acceptance_criteria: ai-analysis-view.tsx 'Last run' date uses a consistent format that produces identical output on server and client (e.g., toISOString().slice(0, 10) for YYYY-MM-DD, or Intl.DateTimeFormat with explicit locale)
No hydration mismatch warning in browser console for the AI analysis date

patterns_to_follow: Use toISOString().slice(0, 10) for deterministic YYYY-MM-DD output, or new Intl.DateTimeFormat('en-US', { ... }) with explicit locale
learnings_relevant: Hydration mismatch: server renders as YYYY/MM/DD, client as M/D/YYYY due to different locales

tasks.special_instructions: The bug is at line ~194: `new Date(lastAt).toLocaleDateString()`. This produces locale-dependent output. Replace with `new Date(lastAt).toISOString().slice(0, 10)` for consistent YYYY-MM-DD, or use suppressHydrationWarning on the span if a relative time approach is preferred. The simplest fix is toISOString().slice(0, 10).

shared_files.note: Slot 1 owns database.ts and client.ts as they need type + wrapper additions. Slots 2 and 3 do not modify shared files.

testing_plan.regression_reason: Last regression was iteration 112 (3 iterations ago). Cadence floor is every 8th — not due until iteration 120.
